message:
fix(mission_dispatch): destructure WorkflowCompleted event instead of assert_eq! (ft-zv3u9)

mission_dispatch.rs:542 used assert_eq!(event, Event::WorkflowCompleted{...})
which requires `crate::events::Event: PartialEq`. The Event enum is
intentionally NOT PartialEq because its variants carry types
(Detection, UserVarPayload) that would each need PartialEq, cascading
the trait bound through events.rs and patterns.rs and pulling in
serde_json::Value comparison semantics that aren't free.

Switch the assertion to a match-and-destructure pattern that pulls
the three fields out and asserts them individually. This compiles
under --features subprocess-bridge without expanding the PartialEq
surface and produces a clearer panic message ("expected
WorkflowCompleted, got <other>") when the dispatcher returns the
wrong variant — better than assert_eq's blob-diff output anyway.

Verified: cargo test --no-default-features --features subprocess-bridge
-p frankenterm-core --lib mission_dispatch produces 12 passed,
0 failed against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
